articles.title: Combining Senolytics and Stem Cells Shows Promise in Mice
articles.summary: A new study associated with Immorta Bio suggests that combining a senolytic vaccine with mesenchymal stem cells might create a synergistic impact. However, the findings rest on acute, artificially induced injury models rather than natural aging [1]. […]

articles.title: An Experimental Proposal for Blocking Ambient Radiation
articles.summary: A perspective published in Aging and Disease has recommended the use of underground laboratory space in order to remove the effects of surface radiation on biological clocks. […]

articles.title: Optimizing Guidelines Toward Optimal Health Outcomes
articles.summary: In a recently published perspective paper, the author argues that the UK’s official health guidelines on physical activity and protein intake should be revised to recommend levels necessary to achieve optimal […]

articles.title: Rescuing Calcium Ion Homeostasis Extends Mouse Lifespan
articles.summary: Scientists have linked disrupted Ca²⁺ homeostasis to aging in both progeroid and naturally aging mice. Rescuing it with a well-known antidepressant […]

articles.title: How Muscle Loss and Bone Loss Are Related
articles.summary: Researchers have elucidated some of the links between the age-related loss of muscle (sarcopenia) and the age-related loss of bone (osteoporosis). Related in multiple ways Substantial previous research has described the relationship between muscle and bone health. While misloading can […]

articles.title: Intermittent Fasting Increases Lifespan in Male Mice
articles.summary: Restricting food access to an 8-hour window increased median lifespan in male mice by 12%. However, that might be due to voluntary caloric restriction induced by the […]

articles.title: Rejuvenation Roundup June 2026

articles.title: Study Maps Existing Drugs to the Hallmarks of Aging
articles.summary: A new study suggests a way to predict whether existing drugs can extend human lifespan. This method uses a network approach that detects longevity signals in protein interactions [1]. Slowing aging with existing […]

articles.title: How a Primate-Specific RNA Strand Worsens Senescence
articles.summary: Researchers have discovered a primate-specific piece of non-coding RNA that is linked to aging and makes senescence worse. The packing matters While non-coding RNA strands (ncRNAs) do not themselves produce proteins, […]

articles.title: Cell Type-Specific Aging Predicts Disease Onset
articles.summary: A new study has used aging trajectories of various cell types to predict diseases such as Alzheimer’s and lung cancer [1]. This expands on previous research into organ-specific aging. Age is more than one number Gone are the days […]

articles.title: New platform creates digital map for marine biobanks
articles.summary: A new digital platform developed under the leadership of CIIMAR is making Portugal's marine biodiversity more accessible by bringing together thousands of biological resources into a single access point. The Blue Biobanks Digital Research Platform aims to bridge scientific research and industry by simplifying the identification of organisms and biological materials with potential applications in areas such as biotechnology, bioremediation, aquaculture, health, cosmetics and many others.

articles.title: Bacteria turn dissolved uranium into stable compound in 130 days, study finds
articles.summary: Researchers at the Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR), together with Wismut GmbH and scientists from the University of Granada in Spain, have demonstrated for the first time that bacteria can convert uranium dissolved in water into a stable chemical compound when they have access to glycerol as a food source.

articles.title: Assessing lab animals with AI
articles.summary: Rutgers Office for Research (OfR) leaders collaborated with researchers around the world to develop an artificial intelligence (AI) program that has the potential to revolutionize lab research.

articles.title: Engineered ADARs enable precision A-to-G base editing of DNA
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03223-z Bystander edits are minimized by engineering an adenosine deaminase acting on RNA for DNA base editing.

articles.title: Efficient in vivo cytosine base editing using virus-like particles with uracil DNA glycosylase inhibition
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03227-9 In vivo cytosine base editing is made efficient with potent glycosylase inhibition.

articles.title: Wearables to track plant health: Farmers could use real-time information to manage crop conditions
articles.summary: A smartwatch can tell us the level of oxygen in our blood, when our sleep is restless or the number of steps we take in a day. Now imagine that kind of tracking ability for plants. By the time farmers see curling leaves or stunted growth in their fields, their crops may already have spent days under stress.

articles.title: Biotech’s digital twin dilemma
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03211-3 From virtual patients to simulated cells and organs-on-chips, digital twins are forecasting disease and guiding drug development. Scientists now must figure out how to define, validate and deploy these models.

articles.title: High-quality phage assembly from metagenomes with PALACE
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03188-z PALACE combines homology features and deep learning to improve phage assembly from metagenomics.

articles.title: Planting the future: Researchers put AI to work on the farm
articles.summary: Farmers are getting more tools in their toolbox, thanks to new research from the University of Missouri that shows how they can tweak planting practices to make the most of every acre.

articles.title: New CRISPR method makes it possible to control protein production in cells
articles.summary: The speed at which a cell produces proteins is a decisive factor in determining whether it divides, specializes or retains its stem cell properties. A team of researchers led by Professor Stefan H. Stricker, professor of epigenetic engineering at LMU's Biomedical Center and research group leader at Helmholtz Munich, has worked with international partners to demonstrate directly for the first time that the amount of ribosomal RNA (rRNA) directly regulates these processes. Their results were published in the journal Science.

articles.title: Hedera lending protocol Bonzo Lend hit for $9 million after Supra verifier accepts manipulated price update
articles.summary: A second wallet borrowed another roughly $1 million but identified itself as a white hat hacker and said the funds would be returned.

articles.title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
articles.summary: Bonzo Lend lost approximately $9.05 million after an attacker exploited a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract on the Hedera network.

articles.title: Crypto IPO market stalls as capital rotates to AI and macro uncertainty weighs
articles.summary: Funding constraints and investor caution, not regulation, are delaying crypto IPOs, according to Cohen & Company Capital Markets' Christian Lopez.

articles.title: Bitcoin, ether ETFs snap eight-week outflow streaks with $282 million combined inflow
articles.summary: The preceding eight weeks drained a combined $9.46 billion from the two groups, meaning this week’s rebound recovered only about 3% of those outflows.

articles.title: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
articles.summary: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.

articles.title: Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market: Jamie Coutts, Real Vision
articles.summary: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts said it's far too early to call $1 million by 2030, but he's confident Bitcoin could climb up to $250,000 over the next couple of years.

articles.title: Ethereum climbs 3% on tokenization boom: Can bulls push ETH price past $1,800?
articles.summary: Despite strong tokenization gains and institutional accumulation, weak onchain and derivatives data leaves ETH vulnerable to a $1,700 retest.

articles.title: Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital sold about half of its BTC stack
articles.summary: It's a sign of the times as the troubled company swaps its bitcoin treasury ambitions for AI data centers.

articles.title: AI found an Ethereum bug that could take validators offline, but humans had to prove it
articles.summary: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.

articles.title: Bonzo Lend loses $9M in oracle exploit on Hedera
articles.summary: An attacker inflated the value of SAUCE collateral and borrowed $9 million from Bonzo Lend through a flaw in Supra’s onchain oracle verifier.
